revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲山寺 (岡崎市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲山寺 (岡崎市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲山寺 (岡崎市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲山寺 (岡崎市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Okazaki-Kozanji-1.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|qid=Q855964|1=Tendai}}|prefecture={{ill|Okazaki|qid=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.96186111111111|N|137.16663888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kōzanji''' (甲山寺, こうざんじ) is a {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai|1=Tiantai}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Rokku, Okazaki|ja|六供町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokku, Okazaki|1=Q16768955|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16768955}}, {{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|qid=Q242783|lt=Okazaki|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It was established during the Sengoku period to protect the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeastern unlucky direction) of {{ill|Okazaki Castle|en|Okazaki Castle|qid=Q1013445|lt=Okazaki Castle|1=Okazaki Castle}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/539 |title=Kōzanji |website=Okazaki Outing Guide |publisher=Okazaki City Tourism Association |date= |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}</ref>
== Overview == In {{ill|Kyōroku|en|Kyōroku|qid=Q1193418|lt=Kyōroku|1=Kyōroku}} 3 (1530), {{ill|Matsudaira Kiyoyasu|en|Matsudaira Kiyoyasu|qid=Q1041434|lt=Matsudaira Kiyoyasu|1=Matsudaira Kiyoyasu}} relocated a Yakushi Hall and six associated lodgings from Anjō to this location to protect the kimon of Okazaki Castle. Later, {{ill|Matsudaira Hirotada|en|Matsudaira Hirotada|qid=Q1064935|lt=Matsudaira Hirotada|1=Matsudaira Hirotada}}, concerned over his lack of children, prayed at Yakushiyama of {{ill|Hōrai-ji|en|Hōrai-ji|qid=Q11675275|lt=Hōrai-ji Temple|1=Hōrai-ji}}. On the 26th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21096812}} 11 (1542), he was blessed with a son. To commemorate this event, Hirotada designated Kōzanji as the Matsudaira family's prayer site. In Tenbun 13 (1544), the six lodgings of Hōshōji Temple in Wada Village were relocated here, and a new Goma Hall was built as the temple's central structure. This is regarded as the temple's founding year.<ref name="okazaki-rekishiteki-98">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=Okazaki Historic Landscape Improvement Plan, Chapter 1 |publisher=Okazaki City |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}</ref>
In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 8 (1603), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} rebuilt the main hall. On the 18th day of the 8th month of the same year, he granted an endowment of 250 koku of red-seal land distributed among areas such as Roku, Sugō, and Hachiman territories.
From {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 15 (1702) to Genroku 16 (1703), {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qid=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}} rebuilt the main hall. Various daimyō contributed by dedicating statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, his two attendants, and the Twelve Heavenly Generals. The hall, constructed in the Zen style, measures five bays in both width and depth and has a tiled roof.<ref name="okazaki-rekishiteki-98"/> During the Edo period, the temple had six lodgings: Tōenbō, Jōkōbō, Misshōbō, Tahōbō, Gokurakubō, and Kazōbō, and it served as a branch of [[Kan'ei-ji|Kan'ei-ji Temple]] in Edo.
In {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|qid=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}} 1 (1744), a hall was built on the grounds of the temple’s Tahōbō lodging (now the parking lot of the Okazaki Civic Center). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 12 (1879), this hall was relocated to the Gokurakubō precincts, where it is now called the Akiba Hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=岡崎 史跡と文化財めぐり|publisher=Okazaki City Office|pages=60-61}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The following items are designated cultural properties of Okazaki City:<ref name="okazaki-bunkazai">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html |title=List of Cultural Properties in Okazaki City |publisher=Okazaki City |date=2023-7-12 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" ! Designation !! Type !! Period !! Date of Designation |- | Kōzanji Main Hall (Goma Hall, includes ridge tags) || Building || 1702–1703 || 2003 (Heisei 15) December 20 |- | Silk-painted Mandalas of the Two Realms || style="text-align:center" | Painting || Muromachi period || 2003 (Heisei 15) December 20 |- | Silk-painted Twelve Heavenly Generals || style="text-align:center" | Painting || Muromachi period || 2003 (Heisei 15) December 20 |- | Wooden Seated Statue of Dainichi Nyorai || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || Late Heian period || 2003 (Heisei 15) December 20 |- | Wooden Standing Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || 1702 || 2003 (Heisei 15) December 20 |}
== Notes == Kōzanji manages or owns the nearby {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki)|lt=Kabutoyama 1st Kofun|ja|甲山1号墳 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki)|qq=}} (approximately 706 square meters), designated as a city cultural property on July 5, 1972.<ref name="okazaki-bunkazai" />
To the north of the temple lies {{ill|Kabutoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|甲山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the annual festival is held in early October. The festival includes {{ill|Tezutsu-hanabi|en|Tezutsu-hanabi|qid=Q11497105|lt=Handheld fireworks|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}} performed by local youths from the Roku area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1306/1342/p020876_d/fil/04atago.pdf |title=Okazaki Neighborhood Stories 04 Atago Ward |publisher=Okazaki City |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|qid=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: Approximately 300 meters north of the "Kagoda Park Mae" bus stop (bound for Okazaki City Hospital).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=岡崎市役所|date=1993-3-15|page=154}} * {{Cite book|title=岡崎 史跡と文化財めぐり|publisher=岡崎市役所|date=2003-1-1}}
{{translated page|ja|甲山寺 (岡崎市)|version=98883077|comment=Imported full ja history}}
